steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The blank is the main verb of the clause "this phrase ______ roughly to 'it's an old story.'" A main verb must be a finite, conjugated form that can carry tense and agree with its subject.

steps.title: Apply the finite-verb rule and eliminate
steps.content:
The subject "this phrase" is singular, so the main verb must be singular and finite.

A. to translate — infinitive; non-finite, cannot be a main verb. Eliminated.
B. translating — gerund/present participle; non-finite, cannot be a main verb. Eliminated.
C. having translated — perfect participle; non-finite, cannot be a main verb. Eliminated.
D. translates — third-person singular present tense; finite verb that agrees with "this phrase." Correct.

D is correct.
